title: 7-foot Outdoor Table with Bench
summary: This DIY outdoor table is made out of pieces of 2x6 that are screwed together with pocket holes.
description: This DIY outdoor table is made out of pieces of 2x6 that are screwed together with pocket holes. There is a support layer under the table top consisting of cross supports and stretchers. The legs are powder-coated steel from Semi Exact. You can build an optional bench or two to match, or add your choice of chairs to change up the look.

materials.name: 220 grit sandpaper (use any other grit in between these two for a better finish)
Box of 2" Kreg Blue-Kote screws (see tools list)
Powder-coated legs from Semi Exact (link in Extras tab), more if you choose to build the bench

steps.title: Watch the Video!
steps.body: Check out the corresponding YouTube video for this project.

steps.title: Cut and fasten the table top
steps.body:
Cut 6 pieces of 2x6 at a length of 7'.
Connect the pieces to make the top using pocket holes every 8 inches on the under side of the table top.
I used bolts as spacers with the pocket screws going through the center to make gaps for rain water about 1/8" to 1/4" wide.
This table top is reinforced by a support layer underneath it.

steps.title: Cut the stretchers
steps.body: Cut 2 stretchers with notches with tapered ends. See diagram on this step for placement.

steps.title: Assemble the support layer
steps.body:
CROSS SUPPORTS: Cut 3 cross supports with the ends cut at a 30 degree angle.
STEEL LEGS: The legs are heavy-duty powder-coated steel legs from Semi-Exact and are 28" tall (see Extras tab for link).

steps.title: Add the cross supports
steps.body: Screw through the cross supports and into the table top.

steps.title: Add the stretchers and legs
steps.body:
Screw through the stretchers and into both the cross supports and table top.
Screw steel legs to the underside of the table top.

steps.title: Optional - add benches
steps.body: If you choose to add benches to your table, the legs used are in the extras tab. Choose a length for your bench boards that are either the same length as your table (7 feet) or slightly shorter. Cut two per bench, and attach to the metal legs.
